Off Calendar Jul-25-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CARLOS ARANETA'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS AND MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY ACTION.
APPEARANCES: DAVID M. SLOAN, ESQUIRE (650.399.9655) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. LONG X. DO, ESQUIRE AND MICHAEL J. BAKER, ESQUIRE (HOWARD RICE, 415.765.4643) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANTS.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DENIED BASED ON CALDER V JONES (1984) 465 U.S. 783. THE TRANSFERS OCCURRED IN CALIFORNIA. DEFENDANT WAS PRESENT DURING THE JANUARY TRANSACTION AND OWNS STOCK IN A CALIFORNIA COMPANY. THE ALLEGED PURPOSE OF DEFENDANT'S TRANSFER WAS TO PRECLUDE PLAINTIFF FROM COLLECTING A JUDGMENT IN CALIFORNIA. THE MOTION TO DISMISS OR STAY IS DENIED. THE CENTRAL EVENTS AT ISSUE OCCURRED IN CALIFORNIA AND CALIFORNIA HAS AN INTEREST IN SEEING THAT ITS LAWS ARE ENFORCED. GIVEN THAT ALL PARTIES RESIDE IN THE PHILLIPINES, THERE IS NO MORE HARDSHIP ON DEFENDANT THAN PLAINTIFF. RULINGS ON EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS AND REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE SET FORTH ON THE RECORD. MR. BAKER IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/DS-X) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
